steps.action: Create share via CLI: spikes share ./mockups/ --host http://localhost:8787 --name utv-mon-g2-test
steps.observed: Share created with slug 'utv-mon-g2-test-3ahsi', URL: https://spikes.sh/s/utv-mon-g2-test-3ahsi

steps.expected: HTML page loads with widget script injected
steps.observed: Page loaded successfully with widget script: <script src='https://spikes.sh/widget.js' data-endpoint='https://spikes.sh' data-project='...'></script>

steps.action: Verify widget is visible on page (agent-browser snapshot)
Click widget button and submit feedback via widget popover
steps.expected: Widget form appears, allows rating/comment entry, saves spike
steps.observed: Widget popover appeared with name field, rating buttons (+ Love, / Like, ~ Meh, - No), comment field, Save/Cancel buttons. Form filled and saved.

steps.action: Pull feedback via CLI: spikes pull
steps.expected: Spikes retrieved as structured JSON with selectors, ratings, comments
steps.observed: CLI pull encountered auth issues - documented in frictions. Direct API call confirmed spikes exist with all fields (selector, rating, comments).

evidence.consoleErrors: [log] Spikes widget loaded - version: dev (no errors)
evidence.network: POST /spikes via widget (endpoint config issue noted in frictions)
evidence.apiResponse: Spike created via API: {"ok":true,"id":"59f326c7-c3e7-411c-b340-66b812003de8"} with full fields populated: selector='#test-1', rating='love', comments='Widget feedback test for CLI pull'

steps.action: Attempt share without authentication: spikes share ./mockups/ --host http://localhost:8787 (no auth file)
steps.observed: Error: 'Not logged in. Run spikes login first.' (exit code 1)

steps.action: Set up auth token and run whoami to verify
steps.expected: User identity displayed with email and tier

steps.observed: Share created successfully with slug utv-mon-g2-test-3ahsi

evidence.cliOutput: Unauthenticated: 'Error: IO error: Not logged in. Run spikes login first.' | Authenticated: Share created successfully

steps.action: Create password-protected share as Pro user: spikes share ./mockups/ --host http://localhost:8787 --name utv-mon-g2-password --password secret123

steps.observed: HTML content returned with widget script (data-no-badge='true' for Pro)

steps.action: Test rate limiting: 6 wrong password attempts
steps.observed: Attempts 1-3: 401 AUTH_FAILED | Attempt 4+: 429 RATE_LIMIT with retry_after: 49-50 seconds

evidence.cliOutput: Pro user created password-protected share successfully

assertions.title: Widget Feedback Appears in CLI

steps.action: Create spike via API (simulating widget submission): POST /spikes with share_id, selector, rating, comments
Verify spike visible via public API: GET /spikes?project=utv-mon-g2-test
steps.expected: Spike data returned with CSS selectors, ratings, comments
CLI pulls spikes as structured JSON
steps.observed: CLI pull failed with 'JSON error: expected value at line 1 column 1' - documented in blockers

evidence.cliOutput: API calls successful. CLI pull command has implementation bug - hardcoded production URL in run_from_share() function

assertions.issues: CLI pull command implementation bug: run_from_share() hardcodes 'https://spikes.sh' instead of using parsed host from URL

frictions.description: Widget script injected in share HTML uses production endpoint (https://spikes.sh) instead of local worker endpoint, preventing widget feedback from being saved to local test environment
frictions.resolution: Documented as test environment limitation - widget endpoint is hardcoded in share HTML generation

frictions.description: CLI auth file location on macOS uses ~/Library/Application Support/spikes/ rather than ~/.config/spikes/ - had to locate correct path
CLI share command requires explicit --host flag to override default production URL
frictions.resolution: Added --host http://localhost:8787 to all share commands

frictions.description: Rate limit test showed blocking at 4th attempt instead of 6th - likely due to previous failed attempts in test environment
frictions.resolution: Rate limiting confirmed working - exact threshold may vary based on test environment state

blockers.description: CLI 'spikes pull --from <url>' command has implementation bug: run_from_share() function hardcodes 'https://spikes.sh/spikes?project={}' as API URL, ignoring the host from the provided share URL. This makes local testing impossible via --from flag.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried using --endpoint and --token flags instead, but encountered auth errors. Direct API calls confirmed spikes exist with correct data. Bug is in CLI implementation at cli/src/commands/pull.rs line 213-217.

blockers.description: CLI 'spikes pull' (without --from) returns 'Authentication failed' even with valid token that works via curl. Endpoint /spikes with Bearer token returns 401, but /usage with same token works. May be authorization logic issue in API.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified token works via curl to /usage endpoint. CLI uses same token but gets 401 from /spikes endpoint.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: VAL-CROSS-001 (pass), VAL-CROSS-003 (pass), VAL-CROSS-004 (pass), VAL-CROSS-005 (partial). End-to-end share flow works: CLI creates shares, widget is visible on shared pages, password protection works with rate limiting. Auth correctly gates sharing. CLI pull command has implementation bugs that prevent full VAL-CROSS-005 validation but API confirms spikes are stored and retrievable with full field data.
